name: Mara Lindt
title: Brand & Type Designer
bio:
Mara is a Berlin-based brand and typography designer with a decade of experience building visual identities for cultural institutions, type foundries, and independent publishers. She approaches every brand problem as a typographic problem first, and believes that the right typeface is half the work.

She holds an MA in Type Design from the Royal College of Art and has been a lecturer at the Berlin University of the Arts since 2022.
awards: Eye Magazine Designer of the Year 2024

name: Felix Kwan
title: Identity & Spatial Designer
bio:
Felix works across brand identity and spatial design from his studio in London. His practice spans everything from logotype design to environmental graphics, with a particular interest in how brands translate from print to architecture.

Previously a partner at Pentagram London, Felix now runs a boutique studio with a small team of four.

name: Sofia Reyes
title: Brand & Colour Systems Designer
bio:
Sofia builds brand systems for travel, hospitality, and cultural organisations from her studio in Mexico City. She is best known for her work with colour — specifically, her process of deriving brand palettes from satellite and aerial photography of the places her clients inhabit.

Her work has been featured in Brand New, Print Magazine, and the AIGA Eye on Design.

name: James Okafor
title: Creative Technologist & Web Director
bio:
James bridges the gap between design direction and front-end engineering. Born in Lagos and now based in San Francisco, his practice focuses on cinematic web experiences for creative studios, fashion houses, and cultural institutions.

He leads interactive at Instrument and teaches advanced web animation at the California College of the Arts.

name: Yuki Tanaka
title: Product & Design Systems Designer
bio:
Yuki designs product interfaces and the systems that underpin them, with a focus on data-dense SaaS applications. Based in Tokyo, she works primarily with early-stage startups and design teams at growth-stage tech companies who need their visual systems rebuilt from the ground up.

She previously led design systems at Vercel and Figma Tokyo.

name: Clara Bauer
title: Web & Type Systems Designer
bio:
Clara designs open-source tools and typographic systems for the web, with a strong philosophy around accessible, performant web typography. Her portfolio system Bloom has been used by over 4,000 designers worldwide.

Based in Vienna, she is an active contributor to the CSS Fonts Working Group and a speaker at CSS-focused conferences across Europe.

name: Riku Mäkinen
title: Motion Director & 3D Artist
bio:
Riku directs motion and 3D work for global brands, broadcasters, and cultural institutions from his base in Helsinki. He is known for deeply typographic motion work — pieces where letterforms are treated as physical, spatial objects rather than flat overlays.

His work has screened at OFFF Barcelona, Motionographer, and the Helsinki Design Museum.

name: Amara Diallo
title: Interactive & Motion Designer
bio:
Amara works at the intersection of type design, motion, and browser-based interactivity. Based in Paris, her practice is primarily experimental — building tools, demos, and interactive pieces that push the limits of what CSS and the Web Animation API can do.

Her work has been adopted by type foundries including Grilli Type and Colophon as interactive specimen formats.

name: Henrik Ström
title: Editorial & Print Designer
bio:
Henrik is one of Scandinavia's leading editorial designers, with 20 years of experience designing annual reports, books, and print campaigns for some of the world's best-known brands. He works closely with the printing process — specifying paper stocks, inks, and binding methods with the same care he applies to typography.

Based in Stockholm, he teaches editorial design at Beckmans College of Design.

name: Nadia Petrov
title: Independent Publisher & Art Director
bio:
Nadia publishes and art directs Grain Magazine from her studio in Vilnius — an independent quarterly on food and culture that has developed a devoted international readership through its deliberately uncomfortable visual language.

She also takes on art direction projects for publishers and cultural institutions who want work that refuses conventional legibility in favour of something more interesting.
awards: Best Designed Magazine — European Design Awards 2025

name: Leo Marchetti
title: Industrial & Furniture Designer
bio:
Leo designs furniture and objects with an engineer's respect for materials and a modernist's distrust of decoration. Based in Milan, he works with European manufacturers to develop collections that are coherent from concept to production.

His FORM collection for Hay launched at Salone del Mobile 2025 and sold out its first production run in 11 days.

name: Soo-Jin Park
title: Product & Lighting Designer
bio:
Soo-Jin designs lighting and small objects that treat manufacturing constraints as creative prompts rather than limitations. Her Lume pendant lamp — made from a single piece of spun aluminium — has become a reference point for material-first product design.

Based in Seoul, she collaborates with Studio 7.5 in Berlin and is currently developing a new collection with a Japanese ceramics manufacturer.

name: Marco Giannis
title: Brand & Product Identity Designer
bio: Marco works at the edge of brand identity and product design — building visual languages for physical products, where every touchpoint from packaging to digital needs to feel like it belongs to the same system. Born in Athens and now based in London, his clients include independent watchmakers, luxury hardware brands, and contemporary furniture makers.

name: Priya Sharma
title: UX Researcher & Product Designer
bio:
Priya leads research-driven design engagements for health, wellness, and consumer tech products. Her methodology centres on the tension between engagement metrics and genuine user wellbeing — she has become known for designing products that deliberately reduce compulsive usage patterns.

Based in New York, she previously led design at IDEO and holds an MSc in Cognitive Science from MIT.

name: Ben Walsh
title: UX & Cartographic Designer
bio:
Ben designs spatial and navigation products that try to reconnect people with the physical world rather than replacing it. His background spans traditional cartography, information design, and mobile UX — a combination he uses to build map products that feel like they were made by someone who actually walks places.

He is a partner at Stamen Design in San Francisco and a contributing editor to the Journal of Maps.

name: Ada Osei
title: Product Designer — FinTech & B2C
bio:
Ada designs consumer financial products that treat users as intelligent adults rather than anxiety-prone data sources. Born in Accra and now based in Amsterdam, she works with early-stage fintech startups and larger financial institutions undergoing digital transformation.

She is also a co-founder of Designed in Africa, a platform showcasing work by African designers across all disciplines.

name: Kai Nguyen
title: Mobile UI/UX Designer
bio:
Kai designs mobile-first product experiences with a focus on clarity, gesture design, and platform conventions. Based in Ho Chi Minh City, he works with regional startups building consumer apps across Southeast Asia's rapidly growing mobile market.

He documents his process publicly on his website and has become a reference point for accessible, culturally considered mobile design in the region.

name: Lena Fischer
title: Brand & Illustration Director
bio:
Lena combines rigorous brand thinking with hand-drawn illustration to create identities that feel genuinely different from the agency-standard output. Based in Zurich, she works with food, beverage, and lifestyle brands who want a visual language that has warmth without losing structure.

She is also an illustrator in her own right, with work published in Neue Grafik and featured in the ADC Gallery New York.

name: Omar Al-Rashid
title: Digital & Web Experience Designer
bio:
Omar designs digital brand experiences for clients in the Gulf region and internationally, with a focus on Arabic typography and bilingual design systems. Based in Dubai, he works with government bodies, cultural institutions, and luxury real estate developers who need digital work that reflects both modernity and regional identity.

He is one of very few designers working at high level in both Arabic and Latin type systems simultaneously.

name: Isabela Santos
title: Motion & Art Direction
bio:
Isabela directs motion and visual identity work for music, fashion, and cultural clients across Latin America and Europe. Based in São Paulo, her work is characterised by a maximalist energy that nonetheless operates within rigorous structural logic — everything moves for a reason.

She has directed title sequences for two Netflix Originals and creative-directed the visual identity of São Paulo Art Week 2025.
awards: São Paulo Art Week Creative Director 2025
